zh.lifeSummary: Hywel Fychan ap Hywel是Hywel ap Dafydd的兒子，於大約1296年出生在威爾士布雷康史爾坎崔福。他和Joan Havard至少有2個兒子和3個女兒。他去世在英國威爾斯丹比爾恩，埋葬在英國威爾斯丹比爾恩。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Hywel Fychan ap Hywel是Hywel ap Dafydd的儿子，于大约1296年出生在威尔士布雷康史爾坎崔福。他和Joan Havard至少有2个儿子和3个女儿。他去世在英国威尔斯丹比爾恩，埋葬在英国威尔斯丹比爾恩。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): habitational name from Apt, the Yiddish name of Opatów in Świętokrzyskie Voivodeship, Poland. The placename (in German, Yiddish, and Polish) is from a root meaning ‘abbot’, the place having been named for the local abbey. Compare   Abt  .

variants.name: Abt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Horst, Johann, Kurt.

German and Dutch: from Middle Low German or Middle Dutch abt ‘abbot’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a servant of an abbot or a nickname for someone thought to resemble an abbot in some way. In some instances it may have been a topographic or habitational name referring to a house named with this word.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): variant of   Abt  , a habitational name from Opatów in Poland.

variants.name: Apter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Avrohom, Bronya, Eliezer, Naftali.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Apt (see   Apt  ).
English: habitational name from Aptor in Marldon, Devon, composed of Old English uppe ‘up, above’ + torr ‘rocky outcrop’.

nameMeanings.name: Hywel
meanings: A Welsh name. Some write the name Hoel, which they render 'lordly;' if so, it may come from huail, a vice-regent, regent. Arthur derives the surname Howell from Corn. -Brit, houl, the sun; and he adds, high, exalted.' But Hywel is rather from hywel, conspicuous.

nameMeanings.name: Hywel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname from a vocabulary word meaning ‘eminent, conspicuous’; it was common in the Middle Ages and was revived in the 20th-century.

variants.name: Hywell
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname from a vocabulary word meaning ‘eminent, conspicuous’; it was common in the Middle Ages and was revived in the 20th-century.

variants.name: Howell
meanings: Anglicized form of the Welsh name Hywel , or a transferred use of the surname derived from that name.

nameMeanings.name: Fychan
meanings: Welsh: A Welsh name of great antiquity, from vyckan, little, small; i.e. small in stature. Compare the surname Bychan and the Armoric Le Bihan.

en.lifeSummary: Hywel Fychan ap Hywel was born about 1296, in Cantref, Breconshire, Wales as the son of Hywel ap Dafydd. He had at least 2 sons and 3 daughters with Joan Havard. He died in Cefn, Denbighshire, Wales, United Kingdom, and was buried in Cefn, Denbighshire, Wales, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Hywel Fychan ap Hywel est né environ 1296, à Cantref, Breconshire, Galles, fils de Hywel ap Dafydd. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles avec Joan Havard. Il est décédé à Cefn, Denbighshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterré à Cefn, Denbighshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Hywel Fychan ap Hywelは約1296年にウェールズブレコンシュアイアカントレフでHywel ap Dafyddの息子として生まれた。彼はJoan Havardとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼はイギリスウェールズデンビグhシュアイアセフンで死去し，イギリスウェールズデンビグhシュアイアセフンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hywel Fychan ap Hywel(은)는 대략 1296년 웨일스 브레콘실르 캔트레프에서 Hywel ap Dafydd의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Joan Havard(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 웨일스 덴비그h실르 세프에서 사망했으며 영국 웨일스 덴비그h실르 세프에 묻혔다.

ru.Aelfannog, Llan-faes, Breconshire, Wales: брекон ст дэвид-лоуир дивисион, брекуншир, Уэльс, Великобритания

ru.lifeSummary: Hywel Fychan ap Hywel родился около 1296 г. в местности кантреф, брекуншир, Уэльс. Его отца звали Hywel ap Dafydd. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери от Joan Havard. Он умер в местности кефн, денбаишир, Уэльс, Великобритания и был похоронен в местности кефн, денбаишир, Уэльс, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Hywel Fychan ap Hywel nació aproximadamente 1296, en Cantref, Breconshire, Gales como hijo de Hywel ap Dafydd. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas con Joan Havard. Murió en Cefn, Denbighshire, Gales, Reino Unido, y fue sepultado en Cefn, Denbighshire, Gales, Reino Unido.
